meta.title: Gege Mould — Automotive Injection Mold Manufacturer | Custom Plastic Injection Molding China
meta.description: Gege Mould is an ISO 9001 certified automotive injection mold manufacturer and custom plastic injection mold supplier in Taizhou, China. Precision mold making, automotive plastic parts, and injection molding for global OEM and Tier 1/2 buyers. RFQ welcome.
meta.keywords: automotive injection mold, plastic injection mold manufacturer, automotive mold maker, injection mold supplier China, custom injection mold, plastic mold manufacturer, automotive plastic parts, OEM automotive parts supplier, car parts injection molding, injection mold quote, precision injection mold

meta.og_description: ISO 9001 certified automotive injection mold manufacturer in China. Custom mold making, precision injection molding, and automotive plastic parts for global OEM, Tier 1, and Tier 2 buyers.

hero.subhead: From prototype to high-volume production, Gege Mould is your automotive injection mold manufacturer and custom plastic injection mold supplier. We engineer and build precision molds that deliver consistent part quality at global production standards — right from Taizhou, China's mold-making heartland. Request an injection mold quote or submit an automotive plastic parts RFQ today.
home.what_we_make_sub: Custom automotive injection molds and precision-molded plastic components for every area of the vehicle — from large exterior bumper fascias and body panels to high-precision interior connectors and under-hood functional parts. ISO 9001 certified mold manufacturer serving global OEM, Tier 1, and Tier 2 automotive buyers.
home.car_parts_diagram_alt: Automotive plastic injection molded components diagram — Gege Mould manufactures molds for bumpers, door panels, instrument panels, dashboards, lighting, trim strips, and all major vehicle plastic parts
home.car_parts_diagram_caption: Gege Mould engineers and manufactures precision injection molds for every plastic component shown — from large exterior bumper fascias and door panels to interior trim, instrument panels, lighting assemblies, and under-hood components.

home.endtoend_desc: Design → machining → tryout → inspection → shipment — no outsourcing means no delays and full quality control.
home.industries_sub: Our molds and molded components supply automotive production lines across the globe — from passenger vehicles to heavy-duty commercial trucks.
home.industry_auto_desc: Bumper, door panel, lamp, and functional part molds trusted by Mercedes-Benz, Volvo, Scania, and more.
home.industry_lighting_desc: Injection molds for headlamps, tail lights, fog lamps, and full-width light bars — with optical-grade polished cavity surfaces and precise multi-point gating.
home.industry_powertrain_desc: High-temperature molds for engine covers, oil pans, air intake manifolds, and functional under-hood components in glass-reinforced engineering resins.
home.industry_interior_desc: Door panels, dashboard carriers, center consoles, and exterior body panels — grained, textured, and Class-A surface finishes for visible automotive components.
home.special_sub: Four capabilities that set us apart as an OEM automotive parts supplier and injection molded parts manufacturer.

blog.gate_location.title: Why Gate Location Is the Most Important Decision in Automotive Injection Mold Design
blog.gate_location.desc: Where plastic enters the mold cavity controls everything from warpage and weld lines to surface quality and cycle time. A technical guide from Gege Mould engineering.
blog.conformal.title: Why Conventional Cooling Is the Biggest Hidden Cost in Large Automotive Panel Tooling
blog.conformal.desc: Straight-drilled waterlines are quietly adding seconds to your cycle time and millimeters to your warpage. What conformal cooling changes.
blog.conformal.fig1_caption: Thermal FEA simulation of a conformal cooling channel layout for a large-format automotive door panel mold. The channels follow the cavity contour at 8–12 mm consistent standoff — geometry unreachable by conventional straight drilling.
blog.conformal.fig2_caption: Two-cavity door inner panel mold with LPBF-printed conformal cooling inserts installed in the three critical thermal zones — belt line, B-pillar rib cluster, and door handle recess. Belt-line warpage reduced from 1.6 mm to 0.3 mm after conformal retrofit.
blog.gate_location.fig1_caption: A selection of automotive injection-molded components produced from Gege Mould tooling. Each part's gate position was determined through Moldflow simulation — validated against resin-specific rheology data — before any tool steel was committed.
blog.gate_location.fig2_caption: Single-cavity automotive door component mold with optimized gate positioning. Gate relocated from the part edge to the geometric center based on Moldflow warp analysis — reducing maximum pressure differential from 42 MPa to 18 MPa across the part length.
capabilities.cnc_machining_caption: CNC machining center at the Gege Mould manufacturing facility in Taizhou, China. Precision machining of injection mold cavity inserts on advanced multi-axis CNC equipment — every mold component is machined and verified in-house before assembly and tryout.
capabilities.cnc_precision_caption: Precision multi-axis CNC machining of metal tooling components at the Gege Mould facility. Modern CNC machining centers hold tolerances to ±0.005 mm — critical for automotive injection molds where cavity accuracy directly determines molded part quality.
capabilities.tooling_equipment_caption: Advanced machining equipment at the Gege Mould tooling workshop. The facility is equipped for high-speed CNC milling, wire-cut and sinker EDM, surface grinding, and precision polishing — all under one roof for complete in-house mold fabrication.
capabilities.factory_floor_caption: Manufacturing workshop at the Gege Mould / Jingling Moulding Technology facility in Huangyan, Taizhou. The 3,000 m² plant houses CNC machining centers, EDM equipment, and injection molding machines — all organized for efficient mold production workflow.
capabilities.mold_storage_caption: Completed injection mold tooling at the Gege Mould facility. Each mold undergoes final inspection, T1 sample dimensional reporting, and crating preparation before shipment to the customer's production line.
capabilities.molding_equipment_caption: Production equipment at the Gege Mould injection molding workshop. The facility supports in-house mold tryouts with production-grade materials, T1 sample dimensional reporting, and serial injection molding production across a range of tonnages.
quality.inspection_equipment_caption: Quality inspection and precision measurement at the Gege Mould facility. Every injection mold component undergoes dimensional verification using calibrated CMM equipment, digital height gauges, and surface profilometers — all traceable to international standards.
quality.facility_workspace_caption: Manufacturing workspace at the Gege Mould quality-controlled facility. The ISO 9001 certified production environment is organized for systematic workflow, traceability, and consistent quality output — from incoming material inspection through final mold audit and shipment.
auto.exterior_panel_caption: Automotive exterior body panel injection mold at the Gege Mould facility. This category of tooling covers fenders, hoods, liftgates, rocker panels, and large-format exterior fascia components — manufactured from high-grade mold steels with precise dimensional control for panel-gap consistency across the assembled vehicle.
auto.fullwidth_lamp_caption: Full-width automotive tail light injection mold at the Gege Mould facility. Modern vehicles increasingly use one-piece tail light designs spanning the entire rear width — these large-format lamp molds require continuous polished cavity surfaces, precise multi-point gating for balanced fill, and dimensional control across spans exceeding 1,200 mm.
auto.lamp_lens_caption: Automotive tail light lens injection mold with optical-grade polished cavity surface. Lamp lens molds demand flawless surface finish — any microscopic cavity defect transfers directly to the lens, degrading optical clarity and light distribution. Gege Mould lamp tooling achieves SPI A1 mirror finishes verified by surface profilometry.
auto.oilpan_detail_caption: Engine oil pan injection mold — alternate viewing angle showing the mold base construction, guide pillar alignment, and ejection system. Under-hood component molds like this are built from abrasion-resistant nitrided steels (typically H13 or S136) to withstand thousands of cycles with glass-fiber-reinforced engineering resins at injection pressures exceeding 1,200 bar.

about.team_subtitle: The engineers and technicians behind every mold we deliver — combining decades of mold-making experience with modern manufacturing discipline.
about.team_photo_alt: Gege Mould engineering and production team group photo at the Taizhou manufacturing facility
about.team_desc_1: Gege Mould is powered by a tight-knit team of mold design engineers, CNC programmers, toolmakers, and quality inspectors — many of whom have been with the company for over a decade. Our team's hands-on experience spans the full mold-making process: from initial DFM analysis and CAD/CAM programming through precision machining, assembly, trial, and final inspection.
about.team_desc_2: When you work with Gege Mould, your first conversation is with an engineer, not a sales representative. We believe that real mold-making expertise starts on the shop floor — and that's where our team lives.
about.factory_workshop_alt: Gege Mould factory workshop interior — overview of the precision machining and mold assembly floor with CNC equipment
about.factory_machining_alt: Gege Mould CNC machining floor — high-speed milling centers for precision mold cavity and core fabrication
about.leadership_subtitle: Guided by decades of hands-on mold-making experience and a commitment to precision manufacturing.
about.chairman_photo_alt: Company chairman at Gege Mould — automotive injection mold manufacturing facility, Taizhou, China
about.chairman_bio_1: With deep roots in Taizhou's mold-making industry — the most concentrated mold manufacturing zone in the world — our chairman has built Gege Mould from the ground up as a precision-driven automotive injection mold manufacturer. Under this leadership, the company has grown from a local tooling workshop into a 3,000-square-meter advanced facility serving global automotive OEM, Tier 1, and Tier 2 customers across more than 30 countries.
about.chairman_quote: "a mold is not just a tool — it is a promise of quality to the customer"
about.chairman_bio_2_suffix: , the chairman personally reviews every major tool program before it leaves the facility. This hands-on, engineering-first philosophy permeates every level of the organization — from the CAD station to the CMM lab to the tryout press.
about.chairman_bio_3_prefix: Today, Gege Mould operates as part of Taizhou Jingling Moulding Technology Co., Ltd. and shares facilities, engineering resources, and a combined track record with
about.facility_workshop_wide_alt: Gege Mould workshop overview — wide-angle view of the precision machining and mold assembly floor showing CNC equipment layout across the 3,000 m² facility
about.facility_cnc_5axis_alt: 5-axis CNC high-speed machining center at Gege Mould — precision milling of injection mold cavity insert with multi-axis simultaneous cutting for complex automotive mold geometry
about.facility_cmm_alt: Coordinate Measuring Machine (CMM) inspection lab at Gege Mould — dimensional verification of mold cavity and core inserts against CAD nominal specifications
about.facility_assembly_bay_alt: Mold assembly bay at Gege Mould — precision fitting and final assembly of complete injection mold tooling packages before trial and shipment
about.facility_edm_alt: EDM (Electrical Discharge Machining) workstation at Gege Mould — wire-cut and sinker EDM for tight-tolerance mold cavity features and complex internal geometries
about.facility_grinding_alt: Precision surface grinding workstation at Gege Mould — achieving flatness and surface finish tolerances for mold base plates and insert components
capabilities.cnc_5axis_alt: 5-axis CNC high-speed machining center at Gege Mould — precision milling of injection mold cavity inserts with multi-axis simultaneous cutting
capabilities.cnc_5axis_caption: 5-axis CNC high-speed machining center at the Gege Mould facility. Multi-axis simultaneous cutting capability enables precision fabrication of complex mold cavity geometries — including deep ribs, undercuts, and contoured parting surfaces — in a single setup, reducing cycle time and improving accuracy for automotive-grade injection mold tooling.
capabilities.edm_alt: EDM wire-cut and sinker electrical discharge machining workstation at Gege Mould — precision mold component fabrication
capabilities.edm_caption: EDM (Electrical Discharge Machining) capability at Gege Mould. Both wire-cut and sinker EDM are used for fabricating tight-tolerance mold features — sharp internal corners, narrow slots, intricate core pin geometries, and features inaccessible to conventional cutting tools. Essential for producing high-precision automotive connector, lighting, and functional component molds.
quality.cmm_lab_alt: Coordinate Measuring Machine (CMM) dimensional inspection lab at Gege Mould — precision measurement and quality verification of injection mold components
quality.cmm_lab_caption: Coordinate Measuring Machine (CMM) inspection lab at Gege Mould. Every mold cavity, core, and critical insert undergoes dimensional verification against CAD nominal specifications using calibrated CMM equipment. Measurement reports are traceable to international standards and included with every mold delivery — supporting full PPAP Level 3 documentation packages for automotive supply chain compliance.
quality.surface_grinding_alt: Precision surface grinding workstation at Gege Mould — achieving flatness and surface finish tolerances for injection mold components
quality.surface_grinding_caption: Precision surface grinding at the Gege Mould quality-controlled manufacturing facility. Mold base plates, insert backing surfaces, and parting line faces are ground to tight flatness and surface finish tolerances — essential for maintaining consistent cavity pressure distribution, preventing flash, and ensuring reliable mold performance across thousands of production cycles.
capabilities.cad_rendering_alt: CAD mold design rendering — 3D solid model and mold flow simulation analysis for injection mold cavity layout optimization
capabilities.cad_rendering_caption: CAD/CAM/CAE mold design rendering showing 3D solid modeling and mold flow simulation analysis. Every Gege Mould project begins with comprehensive DFM analysis — gate placement optimization, fill pattern prediction, and cooling channel design — before any steel is cut.
capabilities.mold_cavity_alt: Injection mold cavity steel close-up — precision-machined mold core and cavity insert with polished surface finish and cooling channel detail
capabilities.mold_cavity_caption: Close-up of a precision-machined injection mold cavity insert at the Gege Mould tooling workshop. The polished steel surface, cooling channel layout, and ejector pin placement are all engineered for optimal part quality, cycle time, and mold longevity — typical of the tooling standards applied to every Gege Mould project.
cases.mercedes_mold_alt: Completed Mercedes-Benz injection mold tool — precision automotive mold manufactured by Gege Mould for European OEM delivery
cases.mercedes_title: Mercedes-Benz Component Mold — Completed Tool Delivery to European OEM
cases.mercedes_summary: A Mercedes-Benz Tier 1 supplier required a precision injection mold for a visible interior component — Class-A SPI A1 surface finish, ±0.05 mm tolerances, full PPAP Level 3, and facility audit before tool steel could be ordered.
cases.mercedes_challenge: Precision injection mold for a Mercedes-Benz passenger vehicle component requiring Class-A surface finish, tight dimensional tolerances, and full PPAP Level 3 documentation. OEM audit of manufacturing facility required before tool approval.
cases.mercedes_solution: S136 hardened steel construction with SPI A1 mirror-polished cavity surfaces. Hot-runner system with sequential valve gating for optimal surface quality. Complete in-house tryout with production-grade material. Full PPAP documentation package prepared and delivered with the tool.
cases.mercedes_outcome: First-shot approval at customer's European molding facility. Zero dimensional non-conformances. Tool entered serial production on schedule. Customer has since awarded Gege Mould additional Mercedes-Benz component mold programs.
cases.cta_heading: Ready to Source Your Next Automotive Mold Program?
cases.cta_body: Send us your part drawing or CAD file. Our engineering team will review your requirements, match them against our project portfolio, and respond with a detailed assessment — not a template quote.
cases.export_title: 8-Mold Lighting Program: Door-to-Door Export to a European Tier 1
cases.export_summary: A European Tier 1 automotive lighting supplier needed 8 injection molds across 4 part numbers — delivered, installed, and PPAP-signed within 16 weeks after their previous supplier fell 7 weeks behind schedule.
cases.export_challenge: Customer needed 8 injection molds across 4 part numbers delivered to their European facility within a 16-week window — with staggered PPAP, split shipments, and on-site installation support. Previous supplier had fallen 7 weeks behind.
cases.export_solution: Parallel engineering streams, incremental PPAP documentation built alongside the mold build, CIF door-to-door logistics with 48-hour tracking updates, and on-site export management presence during both installation phases.
cases.export_outcome: All 8 molds delivered on schedule across two batches. Zero dimensional non-conformances at first-article inspection. Full PPAP Level 3 signed off before each shipment. Customer ordered 3 additional programs within 12 months.
cases.door_panel_title: Heavy-Duty Truck Door Panel Mold — Multi-Cavity Hot-Runner System
cases.door_panel_summary: A European Tier 1 supplier urgently needed a 2-cavity door panel mold for a next-generation heavy-duty truck platform — 1,100 × 750 mm part with Class-A grained surface across the entire visible face, delivered in 18 weeks.
cases.door_panel_challenge: Customer needed a 2-cavity door panel mold for a next-generation heavy-duty truck platform. Aggressive 18-week lead time with Class-A grained surface finish required on all visible faces.
cases.door_panel_solution: Full DFM analysis identified gate position issues before steel was cut. Hot-runner system with sequential valve gating eliminated weld lines on visible surfaces. Pre-hardened 718H steel selected for 500,000+ shot service life.
cases.door_panel_outcome: T1 samples approved with zero dimensional deviations. Mold delivered in 16 weeks — two weeks ahead of schedule. Running production with consistent cycle time under 45 seconds.
cases.bumper_title: Front Bumper Mold for European Passenger Vehicle Platform
cases.bumper_summary: A European OEM needed a replacement injection mold supplier for their facelift front bumper program — large-format tooling with complex undercuts, full PPAP Level 3, and Cpk ≥ 1.67 on all critical dimensions.
cases.bumper_challenge: Large-format front bumper mold with complex undercuts for parking sensor housings and trim attachments. Customer required full PPAP Level 3 documentation and Cpk ≥ 1.67 on all critical dimensions.
cases.bumper_solution: Four-side slide mechanism for undercut release across the full fascia width. Mold flow simulation optimized fill pattern and balanced cavity pressure. Complete PPAP package including PFMEA, control plan, MSA, and process capability study.
cases.bumper_outcome: First-shot approval at OEM's European production facility. Full PPAP sign-off within three weeks of T1 delivery. Mold now in serial production.
cases.hvac_title: HVAC Blower Housing Mold — High-Temperature PP-GF30
cases.hvac_summary: A Tier 1 HVAC supplier needed an injection mold for a blower motor housing in glass-fiber-reinforced PP — deep internal volute geometry with severe undercuts, 150,000 units annually, and aggressive tool-wear conditions.
cases.hvac_challenge: Glass-fiber-reinforced polypropylene presenting elevated tool wear risk. Complex internal geometry with deep undercuts requiring collapsible core design. Annual production volume of 150,000 units.
cases.hvac_solution: H13 tool steel with nitrided cavity surfaces for long-term abrasion resistance. Collapsible core mechanism eliminated secondary machining operations. Conformal cooling channels engineered for thermal uniformity across the complex core geometry.
cases.hvac_outcome: Tool reached 200,000 shots without measurable wear at first scheduled preventive maintenance inspection. Per-part production cost reduced significantly versus customer previous supplier tooling.
cases.smc_title: SMC Compression Mold for Truck Exterior Panel — Large Format
cases.smc_summary: A heavy-duty truck manufacturer needed a large-format SMC compression mold for a 920 × 680 mm exterior body panel — requiring uniform 180°C cavity temperature and dimensional stability under 1,200-ton press force.
cases.smc_challenge: Sheet molding compound tool requiring uniform 180°C cavity temperature across a 920 × 680 mm surface area. Dimensional stability critical under 1,200-ton press clamp force for a heavy-duty truck exterior body panel application.
cases.smc_solution: P20 tool steel with hardened inserts at high-wear shear edges. Optimized heating channel layout achieving consistent cavity surface temperature distribution. Reinforced mold base with guided ejection to prevent deflection under full clamp tonnage.
cases.smc_outcome: First-article dimensions within 0.08 mm of nominal across all inspection points. Tool completed 50,000 production cycles with no rework required. Customer subsequently ordered additional truck panel tooling from Gege Mould.
news.heading: Technical Resources & Insights
news.subtitle: Evergreen technical deep-dives on injection mold design, material selection, cooling technology, tolerances, and manufacturing best practices — written by engineers for engineers and sourcing professionals.
news.section_sub: In-depth engineering guides on the topics that matter most in automotive injection mold procurement and production.
news.card1_title: Why Conventional Cooling Is the Biggest Hidden Cost in Large Automotive Panel Tooling
news.card1_desc: Straight-drilled waterlines quietly add seconds to cycle time and millimeters to warpage. How conformal cooling reduces cycle time by 20–35% on large panel molds — and when the investment pays for itself in under six weeks.
news.card2_title: Why Gate Location Is the Most Important Decision in Automotive Injection Mold Design
news.card2_desc: Where plastic enters the cavity controls everything — warpage, weld lines, surface quality, and cycle time. A technical guide to getting gate placement right before tool steel is cut.
news.card3_title: How to Select the Right Thermoplastic for Your Automotive Injection Molded Part
news.card3_desc: PP, PA6, PA66, ABS, PC, PBT, POM — a practical comparison of the seven most common automotive thermoplastics, including glass-reinforced grades, chemical resistance, cost trade-offs, and which resin to choose for under-hood, interior, and exterior applications.
news.card4_title: Injection Mold Tolerances: What ±0.02 mm Really Means and How to Specify Them
news.card4_desc: Understanding DIN 16742 tolerance grades, what's actually achievable in production vs. what's aspirational, how tolerance specifications drive mold cost, and how to write a tolerance callout that doesn't waste money.
news.card5_title: Top Automotive Plastic Injection Molding Companies by Region — USA, Canada, Mexico, Brazil, Argentina & Europe
news.card5_desc: A region-by-region guide to the leading automotive plastic injection molding companies. Compare Tier 1 tooling suppliers, certifications (IATF 16949), and capabilities across North America, South America, and Europe — with an honest comparison of sourcing from China vs. regional suppliers.
blog.material.heading: How to Select the Right Thermoplastic for Your Automotive Injection Molded Part
blog.material.deck: PP, PA6, PA66, ABS, PC, PBT, POM — a practical comparison of the seven most common automotive thermoplastics, with selection criteria for under-hood, interior, and exterior applications.
blog.material.fig1_caption: A high-precision mold cavity for an automotive thermoplastic component. Material selection directly determines gate design, cooling requirements, and achievable tolerance — decisions that cannot be changed after tool steel is cut.
blog.tolerances.heading: Injection Mold Tolerances: What ±0.02 mm Really Means and How to Specify Them
blog.tolerances.deck: Understanding DIN 16742 tolerance grades, what is actually achievable in production vs. what is aspirational, how tolerance specifications drive mold cost, and how to write a tolerance callout that does not waste money.
blog.tolerances.fig1_caption: CMM dimensional inspection of an injection molded automotive bracket. The mold's ability to hold tolerance is verified at T1 sampling with a full dimensional report against every dimension on the part print.
